excluded_defs.name: set_pattern
excluded_defs.reason: opened to understand when pattern_ is set; confirmed it sets pattern_ then calls compile_pattern_, but set_pattern itself doesn't need to change

exploration_log.aha_moment: add_flag stores the handler in custom_handlers_ but doesn't rebuild formatters_. The fix is: if pattern_ is already set, re-run compile_pattern_(pattern_) to rebuild formatters_ with the new handler.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could look up whether there's a need to clear formatters_ before calling compile_pattern_ again, but compile_pattern_ already resets formatters_ internally.

alternatives: Add a 'dirty' flag that triggers recompile on next format()
key_decisions.decision: Call compile_pattern_(pattern_) immediately in add_flag() if pattern_ is non-empty
key_decisions.reasoning: Direct and consistent with existing set_pattern() implementation which calls compile_pattern_ immediately after setting pattern_

solve_notes: Added a check in `add_flag()` in `pattern_formatter.h`: if `pattern_` is already set (non-empty), immediately call `compile_pattern_(pattern_)` to rebuild `formatters_` with the newly registered handler. This mirrors the existing behavior of `set_pattern()` which always calls `compile_pattern_` after updating the pattern.
task_text:
Fix pattern_formatter not refreshing compiled formatters after add_flag()

`pattern_formatter` in `pattern_formatter.h` maintains two parallel
data structures: `custom_handlers_` (a map from flag char to handler
used as the source during pattern compilation) and `formatters_` (the
compiled vector of flag formatters used at format time). When
`add_flag()` is called after `set_pattern()` has already compiled the
pattern, it updates `custom_handlers_` but leaves `formatters_` stale.
Any flag character already present in the compiled pattern continues to
use the old handler clone stored in `formatters_` instead of the new
one. Fix `add_flag()` in `pattern_formatter.h` to call
`compile_pattern_(pattern_)` after updating `custom_handlers_` whenever
a pattern has already been set, so that `formatters_` is rebuilt to
reflect the new handler.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: set_pattern
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms that pattern_ is stored as member string and compile_pattern_ is called after; prevents agent from wondering whether to store pattern differently or whether compile_pattern_ clears formatters_ first

thrash_preventing_defs.name: compile_pattern_
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms compile_pattern_ rebuilds formatters_ from custom_handlers_, so calling it again is idempotent and correct

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just the add_flag method in pattern_formatter.h where the edit goes. The thrash_preventing set adds set_pattern and compile_pattern_ from pattern_formatter-inl.h. An AI agent would need to read set_pattern to confirm that pattern_ is stored as a std::string member (and thus !pattern_.empty() is the right guard), and compile_pattern_ to verify it correctly rebuilds formatters_ using custom_handlers_ (and won't crash if called again). Without these, the agent would make extra search calls to find these definitions.
